summary: Backend-only testing for iter 3: FixedExpense CRUD (/api/fixed-expenses) + enhanced /api/dashboard/stats fields and frequency normalization. Added 3 new pytest tests to /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. All 14 tests pass (11 regression + 3 new). Verified: POST/GET (with and without property_id filter)/PUT/DELETE work; _id never leaked; 404 on update of non-existent id; dashboard returns all new fields (total_income, vacation_income, invoice_gross, invoice_iva, invoice_retenciones, invoice_net, fixed_expenses_monthly, monthly_expenses, net_income); invariants hold: monthly_expenses == invoice_net + fixed_expenses_monthly (≤0.01€), total_income == monthly_income + vacation_income, net_income == total_income - monthly_expenses; frequency normalization confirmed (monthly=amount, quarterly=amount/3, yearly=amount/12) by measuring deltas from dashboard baseline.

minor.issue: No server-side validation of category/frequency enums — any string is accepted. Consider validating against {mortgage|insurance|maintenance|utilities|community|tax|other} and {monthly|quarterly|yearly} to prevent bad data that would fall through to _monthly()'s default (returns amount as-is for unknown frequency).
property_id is required but not validated to exist in /api/properties. Orphan fixed expenses would still count in dashboard fixed_expenses_monthly after the referenced property is deleted.

Cascade deletes units (preserved from iter 2) but does NOT cascade-delete tenants, reservations, invoices, or fixed_expenses belonging to the property. Pre-existing but now more impactful since fixed_expenses feed dashboard math.

invoice_* and vacation_income are filtered by current YYYY-MM via string prefix of invoice_date/check_in. If the stored value is an empty string or non-ISO (from LLM extraction), it is silently excluded — acceptable but worth documenting.

action_items: (Optional) Add Pydantic validators / Literal types for FixedExpense.category and FixedExpense.frequency.
(Optional) Extend cascade on DELETE /api/properties to also clear tenants, reservations, invoices, fixed_expenses.
(Optional) Validate that FixedExpenseIn.property_id refers to an existing property.
critical_code_review_comments: _monthly() helper is clean and returns amount for unknown frequency (safe fallback), but pairing with enum validation would be more defensive.
dashboard_stats correctly normalizes per frequency and rounds all monetary outputs to 2 decimals; invariants verified numerically.
FixedExpense CRUD follows same pattern as other resources (projection excludes _id, PUT returns 404 on missing id, DELETE is idempotent).
server.py is now 620 lines — still acceptable; consider splitting by resource (routers/properties.py, routers/fixed_expenses.py, services/dashboard.py) before further endpoints land.
No Pydantic response_model on GET /api/dashboard/stats — consider adding a DashboardStats model for schema stability as frontend consumes more fields.
No regression: all iter 1 & iter 2 tests continue to pass.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed properties/fixed_expenses and clean up in finally blocks. Baseline is captured from live dashboard before mutating, then deltas are validated against the captured baseline so pre-existing seed (Hipoteca/Seguro/Comunidad/IBI on Edificio Central) is not disturbed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 14 backend tests pass. New tests: (1) test_fixed_expenses_crud — CRUD with monthly/quarterly/yearly + 404 on update; (2) test_dashboard_stats_new_fields — presence & invariants; (3) test_dashboard_fixed_expenses_frequency_normalization — delta-based verification of amount/1, /3, /12 normalization and net_income drop. Re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v.
rca of the issue: No issues to RCA — all specified features work as described.
